reasoning.A:
DECISION: Verify: Q2  
REASONING: Volunteering in Round 1 would mean trusting three completely unverified answers (Q2–Q4), which is unnecessarily risky. Verifying Q2 for 1 coin lets me independently solve another part of the password and start building consensus, reducing the chance of a wrong overall password later, while my risk of random elimination this round is the same as if I just passed.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q4  
REASONING: My own puzzle is Q2 and I’m confident my sealed answer is correct, so verifying Q2 would waste a coin. Right now all four questions have only a single, unverified capsule, and the biggest risk in volunteering is that *someone else* is wrong. Historically, Q4 (D’s puzzle) is the only one that’s ever been shown incorrect, so it’s the likeliest weak link. Spending 1 coin to independently solve Q4 either confirms D’s answer or creates a conflicting capsule, giving us valuable information for safer volunteering later. Passing would leave me with the same random-death risk as verifying if nobody volunteers, but with no added information.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: With one capsule per question, any volunteer must use exactly this combination. I know my own puzzle (Q3-C) is correct, and all puzzles so far have been very simple arithmetic—across past games, the only observed mistake was a single wrong Q4 from D in Game 1, with everything else correct. So the chance that at least one of Q1, Q2, or Q4 is wrong is likely well under 25%.  

If I don’t volunteer, history suggests A and B will verify rather than volunteer in Round 1, D will likely pass, and we’ll get no volunteer—triggering random elimination with a 25% chance I die immediately. Verifying this round wouldn’t reduce that immediate 25% risk; it would just cost me a coin if I survive.  

Since the probability the full 4-part password is correct is plausibly >75%, volunteering now gives me a lower death risk than waiting for random elimination and preserves all 4 of my coins if the password is right.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
🔍 Verifications completed:
  ✅ A agrees with Q2-B → now Q2-B+A (-1 coin: 4→3)
  ✅ B agrees with Q4-D → now Q4-D+B (-1 coin: 4→3)

✅ C entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A, Q2-B+A, Q3-C, Q4-D+B
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
